name: Aguascalientes (Mexico)
description: A white field with the coat of arms of the state of Aguascalientes centred.
history: Aguascalientes has no officially adopted state flag; the design shown — a white field with the state coat of arms — is in de facto use to represent the state.

features.symbolism: A white field charged with the state coat of arms, in the typical pattern of Mexican state flags.

The arms reference the founding of the city in 1575, the hot springs that give Aguascalientes ("hot waters") its name, the agricultural and viticultural traditions of the state, and its place within the Mexican federation.
